title: JARVIS Step 17 — Panel Consistency Audit Memo
conclusion_detail: The live runtime surface (API sections, system_map api_key fields, and UI panel ids) is fully aligned with zero duplicates. The only drift is documentation lag: docs/jarvis_docs_index/report.json is audited_through_commit da1f1ee (Step 13) and does not yet list the cache_freshness section added in Step 15. This is labeled and non-blocking.

system_map.alignment: All 22 system_map api_key values resolve to a live API section key. The 2 remaining live keys (online, read_only) are meta keys, not panels.

ui_panel_checklist.note: Step 17 spec used the shorthand 'pCacheFresh'; the actual committed id (Step 15) is 'pCacheFreshness'. No codebase drift — api_key, API key, and UI id all read 'cache_freshness'/'pCacheFreshness' consistently.

docs_index_consistency.detail: The docs index was generated at Step 14 and is explicitly scoped to commit da1f1ee, so it predates the Step 15 cache_freshness section. Its api_sections list (21) does not include cache_freshness. Recommend refreshing it in Step 18.

non_blocking_warnings: docs/jarvis_docs_index/report.json is scoped to commit da1f1ee and does not yet list the cache_freshness section (added in Step 15). Documentation lag only — the runtime surface is consistent.
Step 17 spec listed the cache panel id as 'pCacheFresh'; the actual committed id is 'pCacheFreshness'. Naming-shorthand difference only, not drift.
Large untracked backlog keeps commander_snapshot yellow (conservative by design, not an error).
/money-spartan may report a non-required 404 in the route smoke report.
A corrupted 'hydra ' directory can break bare pytest collection, so the scoped pytest command (--rootdir=tests) is used.
recommended_step_18: Refresh docs/jarvis_docs_index (report.json + report.md): add the cache_freshness api section, add the cache_freshness system_map panel and the Step 16/17 memos to the tracked-data file list, bump api_sections to 22, and update audited_through_commit to the latest JARVIS commit.
